eta.nationality: All nationalities not listed as eTA-exempt
eta.notes: All visitors to Kenya who are not exempt must apply for an eTA online before travel at https://etakenya.go.ke. Types and fees: Transit eTA $20; Standard eTA (single entry) $30; One Year Multi-Entry eTA $300; Five Year Multi-Entry eTA $185 (for US nationals only). Processing time capped at 72 hours (expedited option introduced 2025). Somalia and Libya are excluded from the African 60-day exemption bloc and must use the eTA. The eTA replaced prior visa-on-arrival and e-visa categories — there is no longer a separate visa-on-arrival or e-visa system.

visa_policy.notes: Kenya replaced its traditional visa system with an Electronic Travel Authorization (eTA) system operated by the Ministry of Interior and National Administration (immigration.go.ke / evisa.go.ke). Legal Notice No. 93 (issued 30 May 2025) introduced the Seventeenth Schedule via the Kenya Citizenship and Immigration (Amendment) Rules, 2025, establishing the full eTA exemption list. The exemption covers: EAC Partner States (180 days), 44 non-African nations including Pacific/Caribbean/Commonwealth/ASEAN states (90 days), and 27 additional African nations (60 days). Somalia and Libya are explicitly excluded from the African bloc due to security concerns and require eTA. There is no longer a visa-on-arrival or separate e-visa category.

visa_types.name: Electronic Travel Authorisation (eTA)
visa_types.notes: Required for all foreign nationals (replacing visa-on-arrival and prior visa systems effective January 2024). Applied online at etakenya.go.ke. USD 30 fee. Approved eTA allows 90-day stay, extendable. Holders of East African Community (EAC) passports and some nationalities may be exempt. Not the same as visa-free entry — eTA is mandatory pre-travel authorization for most nationalities.

visa_types.name: Work Permit (Class G)
visa_types.notes: Class G work permit for foreign employees. Applied via eCitizen portal. Requires employer sponsorship, proof that no qualified Kenyan is available for the role, academic and professional credentials. Initial 2-year validity, renewable. Entry work permit (Class M) also available for business investors. Fee approximately KES 200,000 (USD 2,000) — varies by category.

visa_types.name: Student Pass
visa_types.purpose: Full-time study at Kenyan educational institutions
visa_types.notes: Applied via eCitizen immigration portal. Requires acceptance letter from a recognized Kenyan educational institution. Annual renewal required for multi-year programs. Students must register with the institution within 7 days of arrival. Dependent family members may apply for dependent pass.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of Diplomatic and Service Passports from The Islamic Republic of Iran for a period not exceeding (30) Days stay. Ordinary passport holders from Iran require eTA.

Holders of Diplomatic, Official, Special, and Service Passports from The Republic of Turkey and members of their families holding valid Diplomatic, Official, Special, and Service Passports, while on Transit or Stay not exceeding ninety (90) days. Ordinary passport holders from Turkey require eTA.
Holders of Diplomatic, Official or Service Passports from the Federal Republic of Brazil for a period not exceeding ninety (90) days. Ordinary passport holders from Brazil require eTA.
Holders of Diplomatic, Official or Service Passports from the Republic of India for a period not exceeding ninety (90) days. Ordinary passport holders from India require eTA.
Holders of Diplomatic, Official or Service Passports from the People's Republic of China for a period not exceeding thirty (30) days. Ordinary passport holders from China require eTA.
Serving Members of the British Military are exempt from eTA; no specific maximum stay stated on official page
Holders of United Nations Organization Laissez-Passers whilst on official UN Business. Exemption is tied to official UN business travel only.
Holders of African Union Laissez-Passers whilst on official A.U. business.
Holders of African Development Bank Laissez-Passers, whilst on official A.D.B. business.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Kenyan government source. The immigration.go.ke website lists citizenship acquisition routes: by birth, descent, registration (including by marriage after 7 years), and naturalization. No investment route to citizenship is listed or offered officially.

programs.name: Class G Permit — Specific Trade, Business or Consultancy
programs.notes: Issued to a person who intends to engage, alone or in partnership, in a specific trade, business, consultancy or profession in Kenya. Requires documentary proof of capital to be invested of at least USD 100,000. Processing fee: KShs 20,000 (non-refundable); Issuance fee: KShs 250,000 per year. EAC Member States citizens: Gratis. Path to permanent residence or citizenship not stated verbatim on the official page.

programs.name: Class A Permit — Prospecting and Mining
programs.notes: Issued to a person who intends to engage in prospecting for minerals or mining in Kenya. Requires proof of funds for investment of at least USD 100,000, plus necessary prospecting/mining rights and licenses. Processing fee: KShs 20,000 (non-refundable); Issuance fee: KShs 500,000 per year. EAC Member States citizens: Gratis.

programs.name: Class N Permit — Digital Nomad (KEP/N)
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals who work remotely within Kenya for a company registered outside Kenya: under employment contract, as shareholder or business representative of a foreign company, or self-employed serving clients outside Kenya. Must provide bank statements or payslips for last 3 months proving income.
programs.notes: Permit issued for 1 or 2 years, renewable. Processing fee: USD 200 (non-refundable); Issuance fee: USD 1,000 per year. Applications accepted from April 2025 per official eFNS portal. Apply via https://fns.immigration.go.ke.

agreements: EAC Common Market Protocol (East African Community — free movement of persons for EAC Partner States: Burundi, DR Congo, Rwanda, South Sudan, Tanzania, Uganda)

validation_notes: ADDED Solomon Islands (SLB) to 90-day visa_free list — present in Legal Notice No. 93 Seventeenth Schedule (confirmed by Erickson Immigration Group analysis and CDH law firm alert) but was missing from the prior record entirely.

CORRECTED Seychelles (SYC) from 60-day African category to 90-day category — multiple authoritative legal analyses (Cliffe Dekker Hofmeyr, Erickson Immigration Group) of the Seventeenth Schedule place Seychelles in the 90-day bloc rather than the 60-day African bloc. The prior classification was inconsistent with the actual legal notice text.
UPDATED eta.notes to explicitly state Somalia and Libya are excluded from the African eTA-exemption bloc due to security concerns, and to note the expedited 72-hour processing cap introduced in 2025.
UPDATED visa_policy.source_urls to include the Kenya High Commission (London) PDF copy of Legal Notice No. 93 as an additional official source.
UPDATED nationality notes references to include Legal Notice No. 93 citation for precision.
No changes to conditional_access, cbi, rbi, or fast_track sections — all verified against official sources as accurate.
gaps: Maximum stay duration for eTA-required nationalities is not enumerated per nationality on the official evisa.go.ke page — the page only shows eTA-required vs eTA-exempt.
Path to permanent residency or citizenship for Class G and Class A permit holders not stated verbatim on official immigration.go.ke pages.
Class F (specific manufacturing) permit details found in search snippet but not directly scraped from an official page — only Class A and Class G were directly verified.
Dual citizenship allowance for Kenya not confirmed from an official source during this research session.
Five Year Multi-Entry eTA ($185) listed as for 'American nationals' only — maximum stay per entry not explicitly stated on official eligibility page.

No Type A (held third-country credential) conditional rules found: the Kenya Citizenship and Immigration (Amendment) Rules, 2025 Seventeenth Schedule contains no exemptions based on holding a US visa, UK visa, Schengen visa, foreign residence permit, Green Card, or OCI card. These categories were specifically searched and not found on any official Kenyan government source.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder special-status rule for Kenya found on any official Kenyan government domain.

Serving Members of the British Military (item 34 of Seventeenth Schedule) appear in the conditional_access array under special_status basis but the precise qualifying document is not enumerated in the official text — only employment status is referenced.

Seychelles 90-day classification confirmed only via third-party legal analysis (CDH, EIG Law); the raw PDF of Legal Notice No. 93 could not be parsed directly — direct text verification of this entry is not possible without a parsed PDF copy.

outbound_official.note: No official Kenya government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Searched mfa.go.ke/kenya-citizens, mfa.go.ke/kenyans-citizens, immigration.go.ke, immigration.ecitizen.go.ke, support.ecitizen.go.ke — none publish an authoritative list of destinations where Kenya ordinary-passport holders may travel without a prior consular visa. The support.ecitizen.go.ke domain hosts informational blog-style articles on this topic but the server returns empty responses or SSL errors to external clients and the pages are not structured as an official registry.
